Original Requirements: Build the Volopa OOP (Out-of-Pocket) Expenses API System using Laravel 10+ with PHP 8.2+. Three modules: 1. User Management & Permissions — RBAC via user_feature_permission with delegation hierarchy 2. Pocket Expense CSV Batch Upload — synchronous validation, async background sync 3. Single Expense Data Capturing — CRUD with FX conversion and flexible metadata
Product Goals: Enable employees to record out-of-pocket expenses incurred outside the Volopa card system
Provide a delegatable permission hierarchy so admins can manage expenses on behalf of users
Support both single-entry and CSV batch upload for expense data capturing
Deliver FX conversion so multi-currency expenses are normalised to the client wallet base currency
Ensure secure role-based access control with granular permissions
Maintain data integrity through comprehensive validation and error handling
Provide scalable API architecture following Laravel best practices
Support multi-tenant architecture with client-scoped data isolation
User Stories: As a Primary Admin, I want to enable OOP Expense feature for my client so that employees can start recording out-of-pocket expenses
As a Primary Admin, I want to grant management rights to other users so that I can delegate expense approval responsibilities
As an Admin, I want to manage expenses for users I've been granted access to so that I can help with expense processing
As a Business User, I want to create single expense entries so that I can record my out-of-pocket business expenses
As a Card User, I want to upload a CSV file with multiple expenses so that I can efficiently record historical expenses
As any user, I want the system to automatically convert foreign currency expenses so that all amounts are normalized to my wallet base currency
As a user, I want to override the automatic FX conversion if needed so that I can correct any discrepancies
As an Admin, I want to configure custom expense sources for my client so that users have relevant categorization options
As a user, I want to attach receipt files to my expenses so that I have proper documentation
As an Admin, I want to receive notifications when CSV batch uploads complete so that I know when to review the imported expenses
As a user, I want detailed validation error messages when my CSV upload fails so that I can fix the issues and re-upload
As a Primary Admin, I want to revoke management rights from users when needed so that I can maintain proper access control
Competitive Analysis: Expensify: Comprehensive expense management with receipt scanning, but lacks delegation hierarchy
Concur SAP: Enterprise-grade expense management with approval workflows, but complex setup
Zoho Expense: Multi-currency support with FX conversion, but limited CSV batch processing
Rydoo: Real-time expense tracking with mobile app, but no granular permission delegation
Chrome River: Policy-driven expense management with validation, but outdated UI
Certify (Emburse): Automated expense reporting with integrations, but lacks flexible metadata capture
Receipt Bank (Dext): AI-powered receipt processing, but limited multi-tenant architecture

Requirement Analysis: The Volopa OOP Expenses system is a comprehensive Laravel API that addresses three critical business needs: flexible permission management, efficient bulk data processing, and accurate multi-currency expense tracking. The system must integrate seamlessly with existing Volopa infrastructure including OAuth2 authentication, FX services, and client management. Key technical challenges include implementing a granular RBAC system with delegation capabilities, ensuring atomic CSV processing with detailed error reporting, and providing real-time FX conversion with user override options. The architecture must support multi-tenancy, maintain data integrity through transactions, and follow Laravel best practices for API development including proper validation, resource transformation, and error handling.
Requirement Pool: Implement OAuth2-secured API endpoints for expense CRUD operations with proper validation and error handling
Build role-based permission system with delegation hierarchy using user_feature_permission table
Create CSV batch upload with synchronous validation and atomic expense creation (max 200 rows)
Implement real-time FX conversion with 30-day lookback and client commission calculation
Develop flexible metadata capture system linking to existing platform entities (categories, projects, etc.)
Build client-configurable expense source management with soft delete support
Integrate notification system for batch upload completion alerts
UI Design draft: RESTful API endpoints following Laravel conventions: /api/v1/pocket-expenses for CRUD operations, /api/v1/pocket-expenses/batch-upload for CSV processing, /api/v1/expense-sources for source configuration. API responses use consistent JSON structure with proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 400, 401, 403, 404, 422). Form validation requests handle input sanitization and business rule enforcement. API Resources shape response data hiding internal fields. Error responses include detailed field-level validation messages for CSV uploads. Permission checks via Policies ensure user can only access authorized data. Background job processing for async operations with queue monitoring.
Anything UNCLEAR: Two project-level decisions need clarification: 1) Notification channel preference for batch upload completion (in-app, email, or both), 2) Whether FX conversion reuses existing platform service or requires new integration setup, 3) Audit logging mechanism for permission grants/revokes (platform standard or project-specific), 4) Table naming convention consistency (singular vs plural) and soft delete pattern preference (flag-based vs Laravel SoftDeletes). These decisions don't block core development but will affect implementation details.
